Combo CBS: A bundled preset of AMD's low-level chip tuning options; advanced grouping — leave default unless you know what it changes.
RedirectForReturnDis: A CPU branch-prediction tweak used to work around certain bugs; advanced — leave on Auto.
L2 TLB Associativity: Tunes how the CPU's address-lookup cache is organized for speed; advanced — leave on Auto.
Platform First Error Handling: Lets the system catch and react to the first hardware error it sees; best left on Auto.
Core Performance Boost: Lets CPU cores briefly run faster than their base speed when there's thermal headroom.
Global C-state Control: Lets idle CPU cores sleep to save power and run cooler. Best left enabled.
Downcore control: Chooses how many CPU cores stay switched on; fewer cores only for testing — leave default.
SMTEN: Turns on multithreading, letting each core run two tasks at once for more throughput.
NumEncryptedGuests: Sets how many virtual machines can use encrypted memory at the same time; advanced VM setting.
SEV-ES ASID Space Limit: Caps how many encrypted virtual machines can run together; advanced VM security setting — leave default.

CR4.MCE Control: Turns on the CPU's machine-check feature so it can flag serious hardware faults — leave on Auto.
BTHB Core 0 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 0's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 1 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 1's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 2 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 2's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 3 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 3's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 4 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 4's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 5 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 5's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 6 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 6's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
BTHB Core 7 Hole Size: Reserves a private memory window for CPU core 7's internal debug use — advanced; leave disabled.
uCode Patching: Applies AMD's CPU microcode updates that fix bugs and errata at boot — leave on Auto.
Sync Flood on MCA: Forces an immediate full reset when a serious CPU hardware error is detected, instead of risking bad data.
Enable Redirect Shutdown to HDT: Sends CPU shutdown events to a hardware debugger instead of halting — for chip debugging only; leave on Auto.
Enable Redirect int1 int3 to HDT: Routes CPU debug interrupts to an external hardware debugger — for chip debugging only; leave on Auto.
Exception Vector: Selects which CPU fault to trap for low-level debugging — advanced; leave at its default.
LBR: Records the CPU's most recent branches to help profilers and debuggers trace code flow — leave on Auto.
HDT OUT: Enables output to an external hardware debug tool — for chip-level debugging only; leave on Auto.
ABL BreakPoint: Pauses early firmware boot code so a debugger can attach — for firmware debugging only; leave on Auto.
ABL ConsoleOut: Prints early firmware boot messages to a debug console — for firmware debugging only; leave on Auto.
HDT Breakpoint 1 (x86 AGESA start): Halts the boot when AMD's startup code begins, so a debugger can step in — debugging only; leave on Auto.
HDT Breakpoint 2 (Before PCI init): Halts the boot just before devices are set up, so a debugger can step in — debugging only; leave on Auto.
HDT Breakpoint 3 (Before OS): Halts the boot right before the operating system loads, so a debugger can step in — debugging only; leave on Auto.
Core Watchdog Timer Disable: Turns off the safety timer that resets a CPU core if it hangs — leave on Auto so the safety net stays active.
ACPI WHEA: Lets the system report hardware errors to the OS so it can log and act on them — handy to leave enabled.
L3 way locking: Reserves part of the shared L3 cache for specific uses — advanced tuning; leave on Auto.
L2 Range Lock Enable: Pins a chosen memory range into the L2 cache so it stays fast — advanced tuning; leave on Auto.
L3 Range Lock Enable: Pins a chosen memory range into the shared L3 cache so it stays fast — advanced tuning; leave on Auto.
RdRand: Enables the CPU's built-in hardware random-number instruction used by security software — leave on Auto.
RdSeed: Enables the CPU's high-quality hardware random-seed instruction used for cryptography — leave on Auto.
SMEE: Turns on hardware memory encryption so RAM contents are scrambled against physical snooping — leave on Auto.
SMM Lock: Locks down the protected system-management firmware mode so it can't be tampered with — leave on Auto.
Report Responsible Victim: Logs which cache line was evicted when a conflict occurs — for low-level debugging; leave on Auto.
Report Shared Victim: Logs evictions of cache lines shared between cores — for low-level debugging; leave on Auto.
Spec DRAM Rd Dis: Stops the CPU from speculatively pre-reading memory it might need — a tuning/debug knob; leave on Auto.
CacheLock Disable: Turns off the CPU's ability to lock cache lines during certain operations — advanced; leave on Auto.
SpecLockMap Disable: Disables a speculative-execution optimization for locked operations — tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
Disable uop Cache: Switches off the cache of pre-decoded instructions that speeds the CPU up — leave on Auto for best speed.
Disable L1 Region HW Prefetcher: Turns off one of the CPU's automatic data pre-fetchers for the L1 cache — leave on Auto for best speed.
Disable L1 Stride HW Prefetcher: Turns off the L1 pre-fetcher that spots regular memory-access patterns — leave on Auto for best speed.
Disable L1 Stream HW Prefetcher: Turns off the L1 pre-fetcher that pulls in streaming sequential data — leave on Auto for best speed.
Disable L1 HW Prefetcher: Turns off automatic data pre-fetching into the fastest L1 cache — leave on Auto for best speed.
Disable MemRenaming: Turns off a CPU trick that reuses memory results to run faster — leave on Auto for best speed.
Disable Recent: Disables a recent-history optimization in the CPU's memory unit — advanced tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
Disable L2 Pf Low Arb Pri: Changes how L2 pre-fetch requests compete for priority — advanced tuning; leave on Auto.
L2 Stream Prefetcher: Lets the L2 cache pull in sequential streaming data ahead of time for speed — leave on Auto.
L2 Stride Prefetcher: Lets the L2 cache spot regular access patterns and fetch ahead for speed — leave on Auto.
L2 Max Stride Distance: Sets how far ahead the L2 pattern pre-fetcher reaches — advanced tuning; leave at default.
Page Pf Hint Dis: Disables memory-page hints that guide pre-fetching — advanced tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
L2 Max Stream Distance: Sets how far ahead the L2 streaming pre-fetcher reads — advanced tuning; leave at default.
L2 Page Pf Distance: Sets how far within a memory page the L2 pre-fetcher looks ahead — advanced tuning; leave at default.
Stream Throttle Control: Tunes how aggressively the CPU throttles streaming pre-fetch traffic — advanced; leave on Auto.
Disable Spec TLB Reload: Stops the CPU speculatively reloading its address-translation cache — tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
Stack Engine Disable: Turns off a CPU unit that speeds up stack push/pop operations — leave on Auto for best speed.
Mem File Disable: Disables a buffer that forwards memory results quickly between instructions — leave on Auto for best speed.
Mesh Gate Level: Controls how much of the chip's internal interconnect can idle to save power — advanced; leave on Auto.
DelayedWakeEn: Lets the chip briefly delay waking idle parts to save more power — advanced tuning; leave on Auto.
Delayed Wake Count: Sets how long idle chip parts wait before waking — advanced power tuning; leave at default.
Mesh Gate Hold: Sets a hold time for the chip's interconnect power-gating logic — advanced tuning; leave at default.
L2EvictForceDcBackprobe: Forces an extra cache check when L2 lines are evicted — low-level tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
L2WcbXrqSerializeTidAgnostic: Changes ordering of certain L2 write-buffer requests — low-level tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
DisableLsReqPfPromotion: Stops load requests from being promoted into pre-fetches — advanced tuning/debug; leave on Auto.
TdPickThrottleEnable: Lets the CPU throttle how fast it picks work to schedule under stress — advanced; leave on Auto.
DisableAllEarlyVbTokenRet: Tweaks low-level instruction-scheduling timing inside the core — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
L2 Up Down Prefetcher: Lets the L2 cache guess and fetch data you'll likely need next, in both directions, to speed things up.
FP_CFG: MSC_CAC_TH: Sets a threshold for the math (floating-point) unit's power estimator — advanced; leave default.
FP_CFG: RET_DISACCF: Disables a timing accelerator in the math unit's instruction-finish stage — for debugging only; leave default.
FP_CFG: RET_ADVTOK_RET: Controls how early the math unit retires its work tokens — advanced tuning; leave default.
FP_CFG: SCE_THROTTLE_X87: Lets the chip throttle old-style x87 math instructions to manage power — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_ENABLE_RRT: Enables a current-control feature in the math unit to smooth power spikes — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_ALWAYS_THROTTLE: Forces constant throttling of the math unit at a fixed level — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_LIVELOCK_THRESH: Sets how long the math unit waits before breaking a stuck (livelocked) state — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_TH_ENABLE: Turns on protection against sudden current surges in the math unit — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_INJ_F_ENABLE: Test feature that injects fake current events to validate surge protection — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_INJ_R_ENABLE: Test feature that injects current-recovery events to validate surge protection — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_TH_SCHEME: Chooses whether current-surge protection reacts live or uses preset limits — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_TIMECONST: Sets how quickly the current-surge protection responds — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_TH_THRESH: Sets how aggressively current-surge protection kicks in — advanced; leave on Auto.
FP_CFG: SCE_DIDT_INJ_THRESH: Sets the strength of injected test current events for surge protection — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
L2 Stream Throttle Disable: Turns off the brake on L2 cache prefetch streams — advanced; leave on Auto.
BP Prefetch Disable: Turns off branch-predictor prefetching, which preloads code the CPU expects to run — advanced; leave on Auto.
BP Disable: Turns off branch prediction, the CPU's guessing of which way code will go — slows things down; leave on Auto.
OC Token Bypass Disable: Disables a shortcut in how the core dispatches instructions — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
OcInterPickGroupFusionDisable: Disables fusing instruction-pick groups in the core's front end — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
EX_CFG: FORCE_EXSCBUSY_GATER: Forces a clock-saving gate in the core's execution unit — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
TW_CFG: CohMemTypesOnly: Restricts a unit to handle only cache-coherent memory types — advanced; leave on Auto.
Locks to only schedule non-spec: Makes the core run locked instructions only after they're certain, not on a guess — advanced; leave on Auto.
ResyncOoOLdOnL1Evict: Forces a re-sync when an out-of-order load loses its L1 cache line — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
MemFile2EGS Disable: Disables a memory-forwarding fast path in the core — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
SpecWcReq Disable: Blocks speculative write-combining memory requests in the core — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
ForceAllSbex: Forces a particular store-buffer behavior in the core — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
DisLiveLockIgnMabIdxBlk: Tweaks how the core escapes a stuck memory-access state — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
DisStIgnMabIdxBlk: Tweaks store handling in the core's memory-access tracking — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
DisDcTagPaDcVFatalErr: Disables a fatal-error trigger for a data-cache tag mismatch — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
WrSzLkUpDis: Disables a write-size lookup step in the core's memory path — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
No Data Access Enable: Enables a mode where certain accesses skip touching data — advanced; leave on Auto.
LBR Extension Enable: Turns on extended last-branch records, a CPU trace feature for profilers and debuggers — leave on Auto.
Disable Page Coalescing: Stops the chip from merging small memory pages into bigger ones — advanced; leave on Auto.
Enable Forced Page Coalescing: Forces small memory pages to be merged into bigger ones — advanced; leave on Auto.
Local APIC Mode: Picks how each CPU core's interrupt controller is addressed; x2APIC scales better on many-core chips. Auto is safe.
Core Dynamic Pwr Monitor (DPM) Control: Switches the core's power-monitoring tuning between automatic and manual — leave on Auto.
Core DPM Enable: Turns on the core's dynamic power monitor that tracks and manages power use — leave enabled.
Core MSR Access Enable: Allows software to read/write certain core control registers — advanced; leave default.
BTC Control: Controls a CPU performance-boost tuning feature — advanced; leave on Auto.
ACPI _PSS: Exposes the CPU's available speed/power states to the operating system for power management — leave on Auto.
Memory Hole on Die 0: Reserves a gap in the memory map for device address space — advanced; leave default.
Disable DF sync flood propagation: Controls whether a serious fabric error halts the whole system; the fabric is how cores, memory and I/O talk. Leave on Auto.
System probe filter: Tracks which caches hold what, so the fabric snoops fewer of them — improves performance. Leave on Auto.
Memory interleaving: Spreads memory across channels so reads/writes run in parallel for speed — leave on Auto.
Memory interleaving size: Sets the chunk size used when spreading memory across channels — advanced; leave on Auto.
Channel interleaving hash: Uses a scrambling scheme to balance traffic across memory channels — advanced; leave on Auto.
Freeze DF module queues on error: Freezes the data-fabric queues when an error hits, to capture its state — for debugging only; leave default.
Core master request ordering: Forces strict ordering of memory requests from cores through the fabric — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
I/O master request ordering: Forces strict ordering of memory requests from I/O devices through the fabric — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
Medium grain clock gating: Lets parts of the data fabric switch off their clock when idle to save power — leave on Auto.
Disable MemCancel: Controls a memory-request cancellation optimization in the fabric — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
Disable migratory probes: Turns off an optimization for data that bounces between cores — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
Stop on SPF error detect: Chooses whether to halt when the cache-tracking filter hits an error — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
Disable TCDX bypasses: Disables fast-path shortcuts in a data-fabric crossbar — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
F1/F fetch probes - remote: Controls cache snoop probes for data on another socket — advanced; leave on Auto.
F1/F fetch probes - local: Controls cache snoop probes for data on the local socket — advanced; leave on Auto.
F1/F fetch probes - on die: Controls cache snoop probes for data on the same chip die — advanced; leave on Auto.
Disable SPF ways: Turns off portions of the cache-tracking filter's storage — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
I/O request bus lock control: Controls how the fabric handles bus-locking requests from I/O devices — advanced; leave on Auto.
IOM PerfMon Ctrl - PrbRsp: Picks which fabric I/O event the performance counters watch — for debugging/profiling only; leave default.
IOM PerfMon Ctrl - TgtSel: Picks which traffic target the fabric I/O performance counters watch — for debugging/profiling only; leave default.
Watchdog Timer Control: Enables a timer that catches and recovers from a hung data fabric — leave on Auto.
Disable I/O bus lock involvement: Removes I/O devices from the fabric's bus-locking handling — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
PIE PRQ broadcast limit: Caps how many probe-broadcast requests the fabric sends at once — advanced; leave default.
Limit maximum I/O requests: Throttles how many in-flight I/O requests the fabric allows — for debugging only; leave on Auto.
Max I/O request limit: Sets the numeric cap on outstanding I/O requests in the fabric — for debugging only; leave default.
CCM LL thresh control: Switches a core-to-fabric link threshold between automatic and manual — leave on Auto.
CCM LL thresh: Sets a low-priority traffic threshold inside the chip's internal data network. Advanced debug knob — leave default.
CCM LL cumulative thresh control: Chooses whether a running-total traffic threshold in the data fabric is set by hand or automatically. Leave on Auto.
CCM LL cumulative thresh: The running-total threshold value for low-priority data-fabric traffic. Advanced debug knob — leave default.
CCM LP thresh control: Picks manual or automatic tuning of a low-power traffic threshold in the chip's data network. Leave on Auto.
CCM LP thresh: Sets the low-power traffic threshold inside the data fabric. Advanced debug knob — leave default.
CCM LP cumulative thresh control: Manual or automatic control of a running-total low-power data-fabric threshold. Leave on Auto.
CCM LP cumulative thresh: The running-total low-power threshold value for internal data traffic. Advanced debug knob — leave default.
CCM bypass control: Lets data skip a stage of the chip's internal traffic router. For engineers debugging the fabric — leave on Auto.
IOS resp bypass control: Lets I/O responses take a shortcut through the chip's internal network. Debugging only — leave on Auto.
CS bypass control: Lets memory traffic skip a step in the chip's internal data router. Debugging only — leave on Auto.
TgtStart control: Tweaks timing of how data-fabric transactions begin. Low-level debug knob — leave on Auto.
PC6 hint FCH GMI prop ctrl: Controls how deep-sleep hints travel across an internal chip link. Advanced power-tuning knob — leave on Auto.
DF picker load step control: Adjusts how the data fabric ramps its workload selection. Engineering debug knob — leave on Auto.
DF clock gating control: Lets idle parts of the chip's data network shut off their clock to save power. Best left on Auto.
DF Pstate change quiesce ctrl: Controls a brief pause while the data fabric changes speed states. Advanced knob — leave on Auto.
DF Cstate clk pwr down ctrl: Lets the data fabric power down its clock when the system is idle to save energy. Leave on Auto.
DF Cstate self refresh ctrl: Lets memory keep itself refreshed while the data fabric idles, saving power. Leave on Auto.
DF Cstate GMI pwr dn ctrl: Lets an internal chip-to-chip link power down while idle to save energy. Leave on Auto.
DF Cstate xGMI pwr dn ctrl: Lets the external socket-to-socket link power down while idle to save energy. Leave on Auto.
GMI / xGMI link error injection: Deliberately injects errors on internal chip links to test error handling. For testing only — leave default.
Reduce credits - request: Throttles how many requests the data fabric allows in flight at once. Debug-tuning knob — leave on Auto.
Reduce credits - probe: Throttles how many cache-probe messages the data fabric allows at once. Debug-tuning knob — leave on Auto.
Reduce credits - response: Throttles how many responses the data fabric allows in flight at once. Debug-tuning knob — leave on Auto.
Disable load-step controls: Turns off the data fabric's gradual workload-ramping logic. For debugging only — leave on Auto.
DBGU_DF trace to DRAM: Records data-fabric activity into memory for engineers to inspect. Eats RAM — leave disabled unless debugging.
DF TCB IOMS trace to DRAM: Logs I/O master traffic from the data fabric into memory for analysis. Debugging only — leave disabled.
DF TCB IOCS trace to DRAM: Logs I/O slave traffic from the data fabric into memory for analysis. Debugging only — leave disabled.
CPU Masters receive probe responses: Controls whether CPU cores get cache-coherency probe replies. Core operation — leave on Auto.
I/O Masters receive probe responses: Controls whether I/O devices get cache-coherency probe replies. Core operation — leave on Auto.
Breakpoint before DF credit release: Halts the chip at a precise point in data-fabric flow for live debugging. For engineers only — leave default.
IOM Rogue Mode: A special diagnostic mode for the I/O part of the data fabric. For debugging only — leave default.
Instruction Based Sampling Control: Manual or automatic setup of a CPU performance-profiling feature. Leave on Auto unless profiling.
Local vector table offset valid: Marks whether a custom interrupt-table location is in use. Low-level knob — leave default.
Local vector table offset: Sets where the CPU's per-core interrupt table lives in memory. Low-level knob — leave default.
Audio IOs: Selects which audio interface the chip exposes for sound output. Leave on Auto unless your board needs a specific one.
Integrated Graphics Controller: Turns the chip's built-in graphics on or off. Leave on Auto unless you only use a separate GPU.
UMA Mode: Chooses how the built-in graphics carves out system RAM for its use. Leave on Auto for most setups.
UMA Frame buffer Size: Sets how much system memory is reserved for the built-in graphics. Leave on Auto unless tuning.
NB Azalia: Enables audio over the graphics output, such as sound through HDMI/DisplayPort. Leave on Auto.
IOMMU: Lets the system safely give devices direct memory access — needed for VM GPU passthrough.
IOMMU Page Table Format: Picks the internal layout the IOMMU uses for device memory mapping. Leave on Auto.
IOMMU Smallest Translation Unit Size: Sets the smallest memory chunk the IOMMU maps for devices. Advanced knob — leave on Auto.
NB Legacy SB Msg Support: Switches between production and early chip-bring-up messaging modes. Leave on the production setting.
XHCI0 controller enable: Turns on the first USB controller so its ports work. Leave enabled unless you want those ports off.
XHCI0 USB31DP port0 SuperSpeed Link Spee: Enables full-speed (SuperSpeed) operation on a USB 3 port. Leave on Auto for normal use.
XHCI0 USB2.0 Port0 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB port to its overcurrent (too-much-power) protection sensor. Leave on Auto.
XHCI0 USB2.0 Port1 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB port to its overcurrent protection sensor so faults shut it down. Leave on Auto.
XHCI0 USB31DP port0 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB 3 port to its overcurrent protection sensor. Leave on Auto.
XHCI0 USB2.0 Port0/1 Battery Charging En: Allows USB ports to charge phones and devices faster. Leave on Auto unless you need charging off.
XHCI1 controller enable: Turns on the second USB controller so its ports work. Leave enabled unless you want those ports off.
XHCI1 USB3.1 Port0/1 SuperSpeed Link Spe: Enables full-speed (SuperSpeed) operation on these USB 3 ports. Leave on Auto for normal use.
XHCI1 USB2.0 Port0 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB port to its overcurrent protection sensor so faults shut it down. Leave on Auto.
XHCI1 USB2.0 Port1 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB port to its overcurrent protection sensor. Leave on Auto.
XHCI1 USB3.1 port0 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB 3 port to its overcurrent protection sensor. Leave on Auto.
XHCI1 USB3.1 port1 Overcurrent Mapping: Links a USB 3 port to its overcurrent protection sensor. Leave on Auto.
XHCI1 USB2.0 Port0/1 Battery Charging En: Allows these USB ports to charge devices faster. Leave on Auto unless you need charging off.
SOC USB S3 WakeUp: Lets a USB device (like a keyboard) wake the PC from sleep. Leave on Auto.
USB PHY ref_clk Stop in S3/S4/S5: Stops the USB clock during sleep and shutdown to save a little power. Leave on Auto.
DBGU_NBIO trace to DRAM: Records I/O-hub activity into memory for engineers to inspect. Uses RAM — leave disabled unless debugging.
DBGU_NBIO Port 80 Tracing: Captures the classic Port 80 boot-progress codes for diagnostics. Leave disabled for normal use.
DBGU_NBIO Port 80 Destination: Chooses where boot-progress codes are sent — a debug buffer or system memory. Debugging only.
DBGU_NBIO Port 80 Timestamps: Adds time markers to captured boot-progress codes for diagnostics. Leave disabled for normal use.
DBGU_IO trace0 to DRAM: Records a stream of I/O activity into memory for engineers. Uses RAM — leave disabled unless debugging.
DBGU_IO trace1 to DRAM: Records a second stream of I/O activity into memory for engineers. Leave disabled unless debugging.
DBGU_IO trace2 to DRAM: Records a third stream of I/O activity into memory for engineers. Leave disabled unless debugging.
DBGU_GFX trace to DRAM: Records graphics-subsystem activity into memory for engineers. Uses RAM — leave disabled unless debugging.
USB DBGCLK Enable: Enables a special clock used for low-level USB debugging. For engineers only — leave on Auto.
GFX CUs/RB Control: Manual or automatic control of how many graphics compute units are active. Leave on Auto.
InactiveCUs: Sets how many of the GPU's compute units to switch off. Advanced tuning knob — leave default.
PCIe Memory Power Shut Down in L1: Lets PCIe device memory power down during light idle to save energy. Leave on Auto.
PCIe Memory Power Deep Sleep in L1: Lets PCIe-connected memory drop into a deeper sleep when idle to save power. Leave on Auto.
PCIe AtomicOps Support: Lets devices do certain all-or-nothing memory updates over PCIe, used by some GPUs and accelerators. Leave on Auto.
GOP Serial Port Log Control: Sends the graphics driver's early boot log to the serial port for debugging. Leave off unless troubleshooting.
Display Spread Spectrum Control: Slightly wobbles the display clock to cut electrical interference; can rarely upset picky monitors. Leave on Auto.
Display Spread Spectrum Percentage: Sets how much the display clock is wobbled to reduce interference. Advanced; leave at default.
Speed Mode(PCIe Port0): Caps how fast this PCIe slot runs (Gen1 to Gen3); lower can help unstable links. Leave on Auto.
Link ASPM(PCIe Port0): Lets this PCIe link sleep when idle to save power; aggressive settings can cause glitches. Leave on Auto.
Poweroff Phy Lanes and PLL in L1(Pcie Po: Powers down this PCIe link's physical circuitry when idle for extra savings. Advanced; leave on Auto.
L1 Immediate ACK(PCIe Port0): Tweaks how quickly the PCIe link confirms entering its low-power state. Advanced; leave on Auto.
TX De-emphasis(PCIe Port0): Adjusts the PCIe transmit signal shape to keep data clean over the wire. Advanced; leave on Auto.
TX Margin(PCIe Port0): Fine-tunes the PCIe transmit signal voltage for signal-integrity testing. Advanced; leave at default.
Equalization Search Mode(PCIe Port0): Picks how the PCIe link hunts for its best high-speed signal settings. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Disable Gen3 EQ Phase2/3 (PCIe Port0): Skips part of the Gen3 link's signal-tuning handshake; for debugging tricky links only. Leave on Auto.
Downstream Tx Preset(PCIe Port0): Presets the transmit signal shaping the chip sends toward the device. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Upstream Tx Preset(PCIe Port0): Presets the transmit signal shaping the device sends back to the chip. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Downstream Rx Preset Hint(PCIe Port0): Hints how the device should tune its receiver for the link. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Upstream Rx Preset Hint(PCIe Port0): Hints how the chip should tune its receiver for the link. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Bypass Gen3 EQ(PCIe Port0): Skips Gen3 signal tuning and uses fixed values; for debugging only. Leave on Auto.
Enable 3x3 Search(PCIe Port0): Uses a wider grid when searching for the best PCIe signal settings. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Enable Safe Search(PCIe Port0): Uses a more cautious method to tune the PCIe link for reliability. Advanced; leave on Auto.
SRIS_SRNS(PCIe Port0): Sets whether the link's two ends share a clock or run independent ones. Advanced; leave on Auto.
SRIS_EN(PCIe Port0): Turns on a mode for PCIe links whose ends use separate, independent clocks. Advanced; leave on Auto.
SRIS_AUTODETECT_EN(PCIe Port0): Lets the link auto-detect whether the two ends use separate clocks. Advanced; leave on Auto.
SRIS_LOWER_SKP_SUPPORT(PCIe Port0): Tweaks timing-marker frequency for links with independent clocks. Advanced; leave on Auto.
Max_Payload_Size(Port0): Sets the largest data chunk sent per PCIe transfer; bigger can be more efficient. Leave on Auto.
Gen2(PCIe Port0): Adjusts a low-level Gen2 link timing parameter. Advanced; leave on Auto.
RX LOS: Tunes how the receiver detects when the incoming signal is lost. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_pre[5:0] GEN1: Fine-tunes pre-shaping of the Gen1 transmit signal for clean data. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_pre[5:0] GEN2: Fine-tunes pre-shaping of the Gen2 transmit signal for clean data. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_pre[5:0] GEN3: Fine-tunes pre-shaping of the Gen3 transmit signal for clean data. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_main[5:0] GEN1: Sets the main strength of the Gen1 transmit signal. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_main[5:0] GEN2: Sets the main strength of the Gen2 transmit signal. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_main[5:0] GEN3: Sets the main strength of the Gen3 transmit signal. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_post[5:0] GEN1: Fine-tunes post-shaping of the Gen1 transmit signal for clean data. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_post[5:0] GEN2: Fine-tunes post-shaping of the Gen2 transmit signal for clean data. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_eq_post[5:0] GEN3: Fine-tunes post-shaping of the Gen3 transmit signal for clean data. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_iboost_lvl[3:0] GEN1: Boosts the Gen1 transmit signal's drive current for longer or noisier traces. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_iboost_lvl[3:0] GEN2: Boosts the Gen2 transmit signal's drive current for longer or noisier traces. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_iboost_lvl[3:0] GEN3: Boosts the Gen3 transmit signal's drive current for longer or noisier traces. Advanced; leave at default.
txX_vboost_en GEN1: Enables extra voltage boost on the Gen1 transmit signal. Advanced; leave on Auto.
txX_vboost_en GEN2: Enables extra voltage boost on the Gen2 transmit signal. Advanced; leave on Auto.
txX_vboost_en GEN3: Enables extra voltage boost on the Gen3 transmit signal. Advanced; leave on Auto.
rxX_eq_att_lvl[2:0] GEN1: Sets how much the Gen1 receiver dampens an overly strong incoming signal. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_att_lvl[2:0] GEN2: Sets how much the Gen2 receiver dampens an overly strong incoming signal. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_att_lvl[2:0] GEN3: Sets how much the Gen3 receiver dampens an overly strong incoming signal. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_vga1_gain[3:0] GEN1: Sets the first-stage amplifier gain on the Gen1 receiver. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_vga1_gain[3:0] GEN2: Sets the first-stage amplifier gain on the Gen2 receiver. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_vga1_gain[3:0] GEN3: Sets the first-stage amplifier gain on the Gen3 receiver. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_vga2_gain[3:0] GEN1: Sets the second-stage amplifier gain on the Gen1 receiver. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_vga2_gain[3:0] GEN2: Sets the second-stage amplifier gain on the Gen2 receiver. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_vga2_gain[3:0] GEN3: Sets the second-stage amplifier gain on the Gen3 receiver. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_ctle_pole[2:0] GEN1: Tunes the Gen1 receiver's filter that sharpens the incoming signal. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_ctle_pole[2:0] GEN2: Tunes the Gen2 receiver's filter that sharpens the incoming signal. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_ctle_pole[2:0] GEN3: Tunes the Gen3 receiver's filter that sharpens the incoming signal. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_ctle_boost[4:0] GEN1: Boosts the Gen1 receiver's high-frequency response to recover weak signals. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_ctle_boost[4:0] GEN2: Boosts the Gen2 receiver's high-frequency response to recover weak signals. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_ctle_boost[4:0] GEN3: Boosts the Gen3 receiver's high-frequency response to recover weak signals. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_dfe_tap1[7:0] GEN1: Tunes a Gen1 receiver filter that cancels echo from earlier bits. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_dfe_tap1[7:0] GEN2: Tunes a Gen2 receiver filter that cancels echo from earlier bits. Advanced; leave at default.
rxX_eq_dfe_tap1[7:0] GEN3: Tunes a Gen3 receiver filter that cancels echo from earlier bits. Advanced; leave at default.
rx_vref_ctrl[4:0]: Sets the reference voltage the receiver uses to tell a 1 from a 0. Advanced; leave at default.
tx_vboost_lvl[2:0] for GEN1: Sets how much extra voltage boost the Gen1 transmitter applies. Advanced; leave at default.
tx_vboost_lvl[2:0] for GEN2: Sets how much extra voltage boost the Gen2 transmitter applies. Advanced; leave at default.
tx_vboost_lvl[2:0] for GEN3: Sets how much extra voltage boost the Gen3 transmitter applies. Advanced; leave at default.
SSC_OFF_FRUG1 [2:0]: Tunes how the clock-recovery circuit tracks frequency wobble from spread spectrum. Advanced; leave at default.
SSC_OFF_PHUG1 [2:0]: Tunes how the clock-recovery circuit tracks phase wobble from spread spectrum. Advanced; leave at default.
AER Dev1Func1: Turns on detailed PCIe error reporting for this device, useful for diagnostics. Leave on Auto.
Bad TLP(Dev1Func1): Logs corrupted PCIe data packets, which the link auto-recovers from. For diagnostics; leave on Auto.
Bad DLLP Status(Dev1Func1): Logs corrupted PCIe link-control messages, normally auto-recovered. For diagnostics; leave on Auto.
Replay Number Rollover Status(Dev1Func1): Logs when the link has retried sending data many times, a sign of signal trouble. For diagnostics; leave on Auto.
Replay Timer Timeout Status(Dev1Func1): Tracks when this PCIe link had to resend data after a timeout — a recoverable hiccup. Leave on Auto.
Data Link Protocol Error status Mask(Dev: Controls whether link-protocol errors on this PCIe slot get logged. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Data Link Protocol Error status Severity: Sets whether a PCIe link-protocol error is treated as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
Poisoned TLP Status Mask(Dev1Func1): Controls logging of 'poisoned' (known-bad) data packets on this PCIe link. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Poisoned TLP Status Severity(Dev1Func1): Sets whether a bad-data PCIe packet counts as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
Completion Timeout Status Mask(Dev1Func1: Controls logging when a PCIe device never replies to a request in time. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Completion Timeout Status Severity(Dev1F: Sets whether a no-reply PCIe timeout is treated as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
Completer Abort Status Mask(Dev1Func1): Controls logging when a PCIe device refuses a request it can't handle. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Completer Abort Status Severity(Dev1Func: Sets whether a refused PCIe request counts as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
Unexpected Completion Status Mask(Dev1Fu: Controls logging of stray PCIe replies that nobody asked for. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Unexpected Completion Status Severity(De: Sets whether an unexpected PCIe reply is treated as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
Malformed TLP Status Mask(Dev1Func1): Controls logging of corrupted, badly-formed PCIe packets. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Malformed TLP Status Severity(Dev1Func1): Sets whether a corrupted PCIe packet counts as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
ECRC Error Status Mask(Dev1Func1): Controls logging when a PCIe packet fails its end-to-end error check. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
ECRC Error Status Severity(Dev1Func1): Sets whether a failed PCIe packet checksum is treated as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
Unsupported Request Error Status Mask(De: Controls logging when a PCIe device gets a request it doesn't support. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
Unsupported Request Error Status Severit: Sets whether an unsupported PCIe request counts as fatal or recoverable. Leave on Auto.
PCIe lanes topology: Chooses whether the board wires up its PCIe lanes automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
Dev1Func1 Engine type: Picks what this connection lane is used for — PCIe, SATA, or nothing. Advanced; leave default.
NBIO Global CG Override: Master switch for power-saving on the chip's I/O hub. Leave on Auto so it idles when quiet.
MMHUB Light Sleep: Lets the memory-controller hub nap during quiet moments to save power. Leave on Auto.
ATHUB Clock Gating: Lets the address-translation hub pause its clock when idle to save power. Leave on Auto.
IOHC LCLK Clock Gating: Lets the I/O hub's internal clock pause when idle to save a little power. Leave on Auto.
IOMMU L1 Clock Gating: Power-saves the first-level cache of the device memory-mapping unit when idle. Leave on Auto.
IOMMU L2 Clock Gating: Power-saves the second-level cache of the device memory-mapping unit when idle. Leave on Auto.
NBIF MGCG: Lets the I/O bus interface idle its clock to save power when not busy. Leave on Auto.
NBIF MGCG Hysteresis: Sets how long the I/O bus interface waits before powering down its clock. Advanced; leave default.
SHUB MGCG: Lets the system hub idle its clock to save power when quiet. Leave on Auto.
SHUB MGCG Hysteresis: Sets the delay before the system hub powers down its clock when idle. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC Global CG Control: Master power-saving switch for the I/O hub's clocks. Leave on Auto.
IOHC CG Hysteresis: Sets how long the I/O hub waits idle before saving power. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK0: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK1: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK2: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK3: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK4: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK5: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK6: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK7: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK8: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL0 CLK9: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK0: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK1: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK2: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK3: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK4: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK5: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK6: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK7: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK8: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL1 CLK9: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK0: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK1: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK2: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK3: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK4: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK5: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK6: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK7: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK8: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
IOHC CG CTRL2 CLK9: Fine-grained power-saving toggle for one specific I/O hub clock line. Advanced; leave default.
SW Device ID Remapping: Lets firmware relabel how PCIe devices are identified to the system. Advanced; leave on Auto.
PBr0 DevFnMap: Maps a PCIe bridge to a specific device slot — for custom lane wiring. Advanced; leave default.
Enable VC7 traffic on USB-HC0: Turns on a special priority traffic channel on a USB controller. For debugging only; leave default.
XHCI0 ComboPHY TX Termination Adjustment: Fine-tunes the electrical signal this USB port sends out. Advanced tuning; leave default.
XHCI0 ComboPHY RX Termination Adjustment: Fine-tunes how this USB port receives incoming signals. Advanced tuning; leave default.
XHCI0 ComboPHY VBOOST Adjustments: Adjusts USB signal-boost strength to help with longer or marginal cables. Advanced; leave default.
XHCI1 USB3.1PHY TX Termination Adjustmen: Fine-tunes the outgoing signal strength on a USB 3.1 port. Advanced tuning; leave default.
XHCI1 USB3.1PHY RX Termination Adjustmen: Fine-tunes how a USB 3.1 port receives incoming signals. Advanced tuning; leave default.
XHCI1 USB3.1PHY VBOOST Adjustments: Adjusts USB 3.1 signal-boost strength for marginal cables or connectors. Advanced; leave default.
Memory Clock Speed: Sets how fast the graphics memory runs; faster can mean more performance but less stability. Leave on Auto.
Tcl: How long memory waits before handing over data it was asked for; lower is quicker. Advanced tuning — leave default.
Tras: How long a memory row stays open before it can close; an advanced timing knob best left on default.
Trcdrd: Delay between opening a memory row and reading from it; advanced timing — leave default.
Trcdwr: Delay between opening a memory row and writing to it; advanced timing — leave default.
TrpAb: Time to close all memory rows before reopening; an advanced timing setting, leave default.
TrpPb: Time to close a single memory row before reusing it; advanced timing — leave default.
TrcAb: Minimum full cycle time for an all-bank memory row; advanced timing — leave default.
TrcPb: Minimum full cycle time for a single-bank memory row; advanced timing — leave default.
TrrdL: Gap between opening two memory rows in the same group; advanced timing — leave default.
TrrdS: Gap between opening two memory rows in different groups; advanced timing — leave default.
Trtp: Delay from a read until the memory row can close; advanced timing — leave default.
TFaw: Limits how quickly four memory rows can be opened in a row to manage power; advanced — leave default.
Tref Control: Chooses whether memory refresh timing is set automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
Tref: How often memory is refreshed so it doesn't lose data; advanced — only used in Manual mode.
TrfcPb Control: Chooses auto or manual timing for single-bank memory refresh recovery. Leave on Auto.
TrfcPb: Recovery time after refreshing one memory bank; advanced — only used in Manual mode.
Trfc Control: Chooses auto or manual timing for the memory refresh recovery delay. Leave on Auto.
Trfc: How long memory needs to recover after a refresh before use; advanced — only in Manual mode.
TSME: Quietly encrypts everything in memory so its contents can't be read off the chips. Auto is fine.
BankGroupSwap: Rearranges how data is spread across memory banks to improve speed for different workloads. Leave on Auto.
Address Hash Bank: Scatters memory addresses across banks more evenly to boost performance. Leave on Auto.
DRAM Timing User Controls: Lets you hand-tune memory timings instead of using safe automatic values. Leave on Auto unless you know your way around.
UclkDiv1M0: Forces a memory controller channel to run at full clock instead of a slower divided one. Advanced — leave on Auto.
UclkDiv1M1: Forces another memory controller channel to run at full clock speed. Advanced — leave on Auto.
UclkDiv1M2: Forces another memory controller channel to run at full clock speed. Advanced — leave on Auto.
UclkDiv1M3: Forces another memory controller channel to run at full clock speed. Advanced — leave on Auto.
Trdwr: Switch-over delay when memory goes from reading to writing; advanced timing — leave default.
Twrrd: Switch-over delay when memory goes from writing to reading; advanced timing — leave default.
TwtrL: Wait after a write before reading in the same memory group; advanced timing — leave default.
TwtrS: Wait after a write before reading in a different memory group; advanced timing — leave default.
TwrwrSc: Gap between two writes to the same memory area; advanced timing — leave default.
TwrwrSd: Gap between two writes within the same memory device; advanced timing — leave default.
TwrwrDd: Gap between two writes to different memory devices; advanced timing — leave default.
TrdrdSc: Gap between two reads from the same memory area; advanced timing — leave default.
TrdrdSd: Gap between two reads within the same memory device; advanced timing — leave default.
TrdrdDd: Gap between two reads from different memory devices; advanced timing — leave default.
Tcksrx Control: Chooses auto or manual timing for waking memory out of its clock-stopped sleep. Leave on Auto.
Tcksrx: Time for memory to recover when its clock restarts after sleep; advanced — only in Manual mode.
Tcksre Control: Chooses auto or manual timing for stopping the memory clock when entering sleep. Leave on Auto.
Tcksre: Delay before the memory clock can stop as it enters sleep; advanced — only in Manual mode.
Tpd Control: Chooses auto or manual timing for the memory power-down delay. Leave on Auto.
Tpd: Minimum time memory must stay in low-power mode once it enters it; advanced — only in Manual mode.
Address Command Tristate Enable: Lets unused memory command lines idle to save a little power; advanced — leave on Auto.
Cmd2T: Gives memory commands one or two clock ticks to settle; two is steadier, one is faster. Leave on Auto.
Data Scramble: Shuffles data patterns sent to memory to reduce electrical noise and errors. Leave on Auto.
On DIMM Temperature Sensor Enable: Turns on the memory's built-in temperature sensor so it can adjust refresh in the heat. Leave on Auto.
CmdThrottleMode Control: Chooses auto or manual control of slowing memory commands to manage heat. Leave on Auto.
CmdThrottleMode: Sets how aggressively memory commands are throttled to keep cool; advanced — only in Manual mode.
Write CRC Enable: Adds a checksum to memory writes to catch data corruption, at a small speed cost. Leave on Auto.
Gear Down Mode: Runs memory commands at half rate for stability at high speeds; advanced — leave on Auto.
DRAM Hardware History Mechanism: Lets the memory controller learn from past access patterns to perform better. Leave on Auto.
DRAM Parity: Adds an extra check bit so the controller can spot some memory errors. Leave on Auto.
RCD Parity: Error-checks the commands sent to the memory module's register chip. Leave on Auto.
AddrTweakEn Control: Chooses auto or manual control of an address-scrambling tweak used with encryption. Leave on Auto.
AddrTweakTMEn: Enables an address-scrambling tweak tied to memory encryption; advanced — leave default.
Data Encryption: Scrambles the contents of memory so they can't be read straight off the chips. Leave on Auto.
Force Encryption: Forces all memory to be encrypted whether or not software asks for it; advanced — leave on Auto.
UMC Key 0: Enables an encryption key slot used by the memory controller; advanced security setting — leave on Auto.
DRAM Fine Granularity Refresh: Refreshes memory in smaller, more frequent chunks to reduce stalls. Advanced — leave on Auto.
CABI Enable: Flips command/address bits to cut power use and noise on the memory bus. Leave on Auto.
DBI Enable: Inverts data bits when it saves power and reduces noise on the memory bus. Leave on Auto.
Disable Data Masking: Turns off the ability to write to only part of a memory word; advanced — leave on Auto.
Disable Address Tweak: Turns off the address-scrambling step used with encryption; advanced — leave on Auto.
Scramble Control: Chooses auto or manual control of the memory data-scrambling feature. Leave on Auto.
Scramble Enable: Turns on shuffling of data sent to memory to cut electrical noise; advanced — leave default.
Scramble Key: Sets the secret value used to scramble memory data; advanced — leave default unless you know why.
Power Down Enable: Lets idle memory drop into a low-power state to save energy and run cooler. Leave on Auto.
Power Down Mode: Chooses how much of the memory powers down when idle — a whole channel or smaller chunks. Leave on Auto.
Power Down Delay Control: Chooses auto or manual timing for how soon idle memory powers down. Leave on Auto.
Power Down Delay: How long memory stays idle before slipping into a low-power state. Leave on Auto.
Aggressive Power Down Enable: Lets memory power down more eagerly to save energy, at a tiny speed cost. Auto is fine.
Aggressive Power Down Delay Control: Chooses whether you set the eager-power-down timing by hand or let firmware decide. Use Auto.
Aggressive Power Down Delay: Sets how quickly memory dives into the eager low-power state. Advanced — leave default.
Phy Power Saving: Saves power in the chip's memory-interface circuitry when traffic is light. Leave on Auto.
Power Down Phy Power Save Disable: Turns off power-saving for the memory interface during power-down — for debugging only.
All Memory Clks Enable: Forces every memory clock signal to stay on instead of idling — a debug/tuning knob, leave Auto.
All CKE Enable: Keeps all memory clock-enable lines active rather than letting chips nap. Advanced — leave Auto.
All CS Enable: Keeps all memory chip-select lines active for diagnostics. Advanced — leave on Auto.
Memory P-state: Lets memory shift between speed/power levels like a gearbox. Leave on Auto.
DRAM Address Command Parity Retry: Re-sends a memory command if a transmission error is caught, improving reliability. Auto is fine.
Max Parity Error Replay: How many times a glitched memory command is retried before giving up. Advanced — leave default.
EDC Enable: Turns on error detection on the high-speed memory link to catch corrupted data. Leave on Auto.
Enable EDC Replay: Re-sends memory data when the error-checker flags a problem, improving reliability. Leave on Auto.
Inject TX Command Parity Errors: Deliberately injects fake memory errors to test error handling — for debugging only; leave off.
Hybrid All-bank/Per-X-bank refresh Contr: Picks how memory does its periodic refresh housekeeping. Advanced tuning — leave on Auto.
Per-X-bank refresh Control: Sets whether memory refreshes one bank or two at a time. Advanced tuning — leave on Auto.
RefAb insertion period: How often a full all-bank memory refresh is mixed in. Advanced tuning — leave default.
Auto-Calibration Control: Lets memory periodically self-tune its signal timing for stability. Leave on Auto.
Additional CAS-CAS Delay Cycles Control: Chooses manual or automatic tuning of a memory access-timing gap. Use Auto.
Additional CAS-CAS Delay Cycles: Adds extra wait cycles between back-to-back memory accesses for stability. Advanced — leave default.
Additional Read to Write Turnaround Cycl: Adds extra pause when memory switches from reading to writing. Advanced tuning — use Auto.
VMEMP Voltage Control: Sets the voltage fed to the graphics memory; wrong values can crash or damage. Leave on Auto.
APUDFE Training Control: Enables auto-tuning of a memory signal-cleanup feature for cleaner high-speed data. Leave on Auto.
