MR3 MP2 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory data-pin register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR3 MP2 Value: The raw value written into a memory data-pin register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR3 MP3 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory data-pin register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR3 MP3 Value: The raw value written into a memory data-pin register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 Control: Chooses auto or manual setup of a memory-chip refresh/training register. Leave on Auto.
MR4 MP0 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory refresh/training register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP0 Value: The raw value written into a memory refresh/training register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP1 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory refresh/training register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP1 Value: The raw value written into a memory refresh/training register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP2 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory refresh/training register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP2 Value: The raw value written into a memory refresh/training register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP3 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory refresh/training register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR4 MP3 Value: The raw value written into a memory refresh/training register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 Control: Chooses auto or manual setup of a memory-chip burst/timing register. Leave on Auto.
MR8 MP0 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory burst/timing register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP0 Value: The raw value written into a memory burst/timing register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP1 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory burst/timing register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP1 Value: The raw value written into a memory burst/timing register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP2 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory burst/timing register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP2 Value: The raw value written into a memory burst/timing register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP3 Mask: Picks which bits of a memory burst/timing register get overwritten. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MR8 MP3 Value: The raw value written into a memory burst/timing register. Advanced tuning; leave default.
Slew Rate Control: Adjusts how sharply power-delivery signals rise and fall. For power tuning; leave on Auto.
CCLK_CONTROLLER: Lets the power manager steer the CPU core clock speed up and down. Leave on Auto.
GFXCLK_EFFECTIVE_FREQ: Lets the chip report the GPU's real running clock speed for smarter power decisions. Leave on Auto.
DATA_CALCULATION: Power-manager telemetry crunching that feeds smarter power and clock decisions. Leave on Auto.
THERMAL: Lets the power manager watch temperatures and throttle to keep the chip cool. Keep enabled.
PLL_POWER_DOWN: Lets unused clock generators power off to save energy when idle. Leave on Auto.
FCLK_DPM: Lets the memory-link clock scale with load to save power. Leave on Auto.
GFX_DPM: Lets the GPU scale its clock speed with workload to balance speed and power. Leave on Auto.
DS_GFXCLK: Lets the GPU clock drop to a low-power idle state when not busy. Leave on Auto.
DS_SOCCLK: Lets the chip's main interconnect clock idle down to save power. Leave on Auto.
DS_LCLK: Lets the I/O link clock idle down to save power when traffic is light. Leave on Auto.
CORE_CSTATES: Lets idle CPU cores enter low-power sleep states to save energy. Best left enabled.
G6_SSC: Spreads the memory clock slightly to reduce electrical interference. Leave on Auto.
RM: An advanced power-manager feature; its exact effect is rarely changed. Leave on Auto.
SOC_DPM: Lets the chip's system fabric scale its speed with load to save power. Leave on Auto.
DS_SMNCLK: Lets the internal management-network clock idle down to save power. Leave on Auto.
DS_MP1CLK: Lets a power-management coprocessor's clock idle down when not needed. Leave on Auto.
DS_MP0CLK: Lets a security coprocessor's clock idle down to save power. Leave on Auto.
SMU CG: Clock-gating for the power manager itself — switches off idle parts to save energy. Leave on Auto.
DS_FUSE_SRAM: Lets the small on-chip fuse memory power down when idle. Advanced; leave on Auto.
GFX_CKS: Lets the GPU briefly stretch its clock during voltage dips for stability. Leave on Auto.
FP_THROTTLING: Throttles heavy floating-point math to keep power and heat in check. Leave on Auto.
PROCHOT: Emergency throttle that slows the chip when it gets too hot. Keep enabled for safety.
CPUOFF: Lets the CPU enter a very deep idle power-off state to save energy. Leave on Auto.
UMC_THROTTLE: Slows the memory controller when it gets too hot to protect it. Leave on Auto.
DF_THROTTLE: Slows the chip's internal data fabric (how cores, memory and I/O talk) when it overheats. Leave on Auto.
DS_MP3FCLK: Lets a multimedia coprocessor's clock idle down to save power. Leave on Auto.
DS_SHUBCLK: Lets an internal system-hub clock idle down to save power. Leave on Auto.
TDC: Caps sustained current draw to keep power delivery within safe limits. Leave on Auto.
UMC_CAL_SHARING: Lets memory channels share calibration data to speed up training. Leave on Auto.
DFLL_BTC_CALIBRATION: Auto-calibrates the chip's clock generator for accuracy. Advanced; leave on Auto.
EDC: Caps brief current spikes to protect power delivery during sudden load. Leave on Auto.
DLDO: Controls the on-chip digital voltage regulators that fine-tune core power. Leave on Auto.
MEAS_DRAM_BLACKOUT: Pauses memory traffic briefly so the chip can take clean power/thermal measurements. Advanced; leave on Auto.
PPT: The overall power budget the chip is allowed to draw. Controls how much power it can pull sustained.
STAPM: Smooths power limits over time so brief bursts are allowed but long heavy loads stay in budget.
CSTATE_BOOST: Lets cores briefly boost faster when waking from idle. Advanced power-tuning; leave on Auto.
Thermal Control: Whether the chip manages its own heat limits automatically or you set them by hand. Leave on Auto.
TctlMax: The top temperature the chip aims to stay under before throttling. Lowering it runs cooler but slower.
TctlMax_Core: The temperature ceiling specifically for the CPU cores before they throttle. Advanced; leave default.
TctlMax_Gfx: The temperature ceiling specifically for the built-in graphics before it throttles. Advanced; leave default.
PROCHOT Control: Whether the emergency 'too hot, slow down now' safety signal is managed automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
PROCHOT Deassertion Ramp Time: How gradually the chip speeds back up after an overheat slowdown clears. Advanced; leave default.
GfxAvfsFtoVMargin Control: Whether the graphics voltage safety margin is set automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
GfxAvfsFtoVMargin: Extra voltage cushion for the graphics to stay stable. Tuning it risks instability; leave default.
Core Dldo Psm Margin Control: Whether the per-core voltage trim margin is set automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
Core Dldo Psm Margin: Fine voltage-trim cushion for each CPU core. Advanced tuning; changing it risks instability.
Core DLDO Bypass: Skips the per-core internal voltage regulator. For debugging silicon behavior only; leave on Auto.
Gfx Stretch Thresh Control: Whether the graphics clock-stretching trigger point is set automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
Gfx Stretch Thresh: When the graphics briefly slows its clock to ride out voltage dips. Advanced; leave default.
Gfx Stretch Amount Control: Whether how much the graphics clock stretches is set automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
Gfx Stretch Amount: How far the graphics clock slows during voltage dips to stay stable. Advanced; leave default.
AvfsCoeffTable_Override: Overrides the factory voltage-vs-frequency calibration tables. Expert silicon tuning only; leave on Auto.
FcwSlewFrac Control: Whether the clock's frequency-change speed setting is automatic or manual. Advanced; leave on Auto.
FcwSlewFrac: How quickly the clock generator ramps frequency up or down. Advanced; leave default.
CC1Dis: Disables a deeper CPU idle sleep state. For debugging; leave on Auto so cores can save power.
VDDCR_VDD Voltage Offset Control: Whether the CPU voltage nudge is applied automatically or set by hand. Leave on Auto.
VDDCR_VDD Voltage Offset: A small up/down tweak to CPU voltage for tuning. Wrong values cause crashes; leave default.
VDDCR_GFX Voltage Offset Control: Whether the graphics voltage nudge is applied automatically or set by hand. Leave on Auto.
VDDCR_GFX Voltage Offset: A small up/down tweak to graphics voltage for tuning. Wrong values cause crashes; leave default.
HTC: A thermal safeguard that throttles the chip when it gets too hot. Best left enabled.
ThermTrip: The last-resort safety that shuts the system off if it overheats dangerously. Keep enabled.
AllocateDramBuffer Control: Whether a reserved memory work-area is sized automatically or by hand. Leave on Auto.
AllocateDramBuffer: How much memory is set aside as a scratch buffer for power-management firmware. Advanced; leave default.
CPU DC BTC: Runs a CPU voltage self-calibration at boot for better accuracy. Advanced; leave on Auto.
CPU DC BTC Set voltage in mV: A fixed test voltage for the CPU calibration routine, in millivolts. For debugging only; leave default.
CPU DC BTC error offset in mV: A manual correction added to the CPU voltage-calibration result. Advanced; leave default.
GFX DC BTC: Runs a graphics voltage self-calibration at boot for better accuracy. Advanced; leave on Auto.
GFX DC BTC Set voltage in mV: A fixed test voltage for the graphics calibration routine, in millivolts. For debugging only; leave default.
GFX DC BTC error offset in mV: A manual correction added to the graphics voltage-calibration result. Advanced; leave default.
CPU Clock Override Control: Whether the CPU speed is managed automatically or pinned to a value you choose. Leave on Auto.
Force CPU Frequency: Locks the CPU to a fixed speed instead of letting it scale. For testing; Auto is better for daily use.
DF Clock Override Control: Whether the internal data-fabric clock (how cores, memory and I/O talk) is automatic or manual. Leave on Auto.
DF Clock Override: Forces a fixed speed for the chip's internal data fabric linking cores, memory and I/O. Advanced; leave default.
Spread Spectrum for PLL0: Slightly wobbles a clock to reduce radio interference it gives off. Usually best left enabled.
Spread Spectrum for PLL1: Slightly wobbles another internal clock to cut electromagnetic interference. Usually best left enabled.
Spread Spectrum for PLL6: Slightly wobbles another internal clock to cut electromagnetic interference. Usually best left enabled.
Spread Spectrum for PLL7: Slightly wobbles another internal clock to cut electromagnetic interference. Usually best left enabled.
CpuCksDisable: Turns off a CPU clock-glitch protection circuit. For debugging only; leave on Auto.
C0ResidencyCtrl: Controls tracking of how long cores stay fully active, used for power decisions. Advanced; leave on Auto.
FAST_PPT_LIMIT: The short-burst power ceiling for brief heavy spikes. Raising it allows hotter, faster bursts.
SLOW_PPT_LIMIT: The sustained power ceiling for long heavy workloads. Raising it allows more sustained power and heat.
SLOW_PPT_TIME_CONSTANT: How long a load must run before the sustained power limit kicks in. Advanced; leave default.
VRM_CURRENT_LIMIT: The normal current the power-delivery hardware is allowed to supply. Advanced; leave default.
VRM_MAXIMUM_CURRENT_LIMIT: The absolute peak current the power-delivery hardware may briefly supply. Advanced; leave default.
Enable Async mode for CCX-DF VDCI: How the CPU cores and internal data fabric sync their clocks. Advanced timing knob; leave on Auto.
GfxCLKFreq Control: Whether the graphics speed is set automatically or pinned to your chosen value. Leave on Auto.
Native Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed used in the standard display mode. Advanced; leave default unless tuning.
Native Split GL Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed for a specific split-display operating mode. Advanced; leave default.
Native Split PVL Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed for another split-display operating mode. Advanced; leave default.
Multimedia 4K Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed used during 4K video/multimedia playback. Advanced; leave default.
Multimedia 2K Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed used during 2K video/multimedia playback. Advanced; leave default.
Navigation 4K Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed used in a 4K navigation/display profile. Advanced; leave default.
Navigation 2K Mode GfxCLKFreq: The graphics clock speed used in a 2K navigation/display profile. Advanced; leave default.
GfxClkDfll: Chooses how the graphics clock is generated and steadied. Advanced clocking option; leave on Auto.
GfxDfllApccEn: Enables a feature that quickly drops graphics speed during sudden current spikes. Advanced; leave on Auto.
GfxDfllApccTrig: Sets the current-spike level that triggers the graphics fast-throttle response. Advanced; leave default.
GfxDfllFreq Control: Whether the graphics clock's fine-tuning is automatic or manual. Advanced; leave on Auto.
GfxDfllFreqMeasInRefClkCycles: How long the firmware samples to measure the real graphics clock speed. Advanced; leave default.
GfxDfllFDelta: A fine offset applied to the measured graphics clock for tuning. Advanced; leave default.
AC Vol margin Control: Whether the graphics voltage safety margin range is automatic or set by hand. Leave on Auto.
MaxAcmarg: The upper limit of the graphics voltage safety cushion. Advanced; changing it risks instability.
MinAcmarg: The lower limit of the graphics voltage safety cushion. Advanced; changing it risks instability.
G6 Mem SS Cfg Control: Chooses Auto or manual tuning for spreading the graphics-memory clock slightly to cut electrical noise; leave on Auto.
Uclk Spread Percentage: Sets how much the memory clock is wobbled to reduce interference; advanced tuning, best left at default.
Umc Auto Cal Interval: How often the memory controller re-calibrates itself for stability; an advanced timing knob, leave default.
AVFS Control: Auto or manual control of the chip's per-part voltage/frequency self-tuning; leave on Auto.
CorePsfTemp: A factory voltage-curve coefficient (temperature term) for the CPU cores; advanced tuning, leave default.
CorePsfFreq2: A factory voltage-curve coefficient (frequency-squared term) for the CPU cores; advanced, leave default.
CorePsfFreq: A factory voltage-curve coefficient (frequency term) for the CPU cores; advanced tuning, leave default.
CorePsfConst: The constant term in the CPU cores' factory voltage curve; advanced tuning, leave default.
GfxPsfTemp: A factory voltage-curve coefficient (temperature term) for the graphics block; advanced, leave default.
GfxPsfFreq2: A factory voltage-curve coefficient (frequency-squared term) for the graphics block; advanced, leave default.
GfxPsfFreq: A factory voltage-curve coefficient (frequency term) for the graphics block; advanced tuning, leave default.
GfxPsfConst: The constant term in the graphics block's factory voltage curve; advanced tuning, leave default.
Telemetry Control: Auto or manual control of how the chip measures its own power draw; leave on Auto.
VDDCR_VDD Telemetry slope: Calibration scale for measuring CPU-core power; advanced board-tuning value, leave default.
VDDCR_VDD Telemetry Offset Sign (positiv: Whether the CPU-core power-reading correction is added or subtracted; advanced calibration, leave default.
VDDCR_VDD Telemetry Offset Value (mA): A fixed correction to the CPU-core current reading, in milliamps; advanced calibration, leave default.
VDDCR_SOC Telemetry slope: Calibration scale for measuring the SoC (uncore) power; advanced board-tuning value, leave default.
VDDCR_SOC Telemetry Offset Sign (positiv: Whether the SoC power-reading correction is added or subtracted; advanced calibration, leave default.
VDDCR_SOC Telemetry Offset Value (mA): A fixed correction to the SoC current reading, in milliamps; advanced calibration, leave default.
VDDCR_GFX Telemetry slope: Calibration scale for measuring the graphics block's power; advanced board-tuning value, leave default.
VDDCR_GFX Telemetry Offset Sign (positiv: Whether the graphics power-reading correction is added or subtracted; advanced calibration, leave default.
VDDCR_GFX Telemetry Offset Value (mA): A fixed correction to the graphics current reading, in milliamps; advanced calibration, leave default.
GRA Group 1: Turns on a custom rule that pokes a raw hardware register during boot; expert debugging only, leave off.
GRA Register Type: Picks which kind of hardware register the custom rule writes to; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA When to Write: Chooses how early in boot the custom register write happens; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Gen Reg Node: Selects which chip node(s) the custom register write targets; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Bus: The PCI bus number the custom register write targets; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Device: The PCI device number the custom register write targets; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Function: The PCI function number the custom register write targets; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Offset: The address offset within the target register space for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Gen Reg Core: Selects which CPU core(s) the custom register write applies to; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Node: The specific chip node for the custom register write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Core: The specific CPU core for the custom register write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA MSR Address High: Upper half of the CPU-internal register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA MSR Address Low: Lower half of the CPU-internal register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA MMIO Address High: Upper half of the memory-mapped register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA MMIO Address Low: Lower half of the memory-mapped register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA PCI Indirect Register Offset[31:16]: Upper bits of an indirect PCI register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA PCI Indirect Register Offset[15:0]: Lower bits of an indirect PCI register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA PCI Indirect Register Write Enable B: Which control bit unlocks the indirect PCI write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA GMMX Offset[31:16]: Upper bits of a graphics-register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA GMMX Offset[15:0]: Lower bits of a graphics-register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA SMN Address High: Upper half of an internal system-network register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA SMN Address Low: Lower half of an internal system-network register address for the custom write; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA DF Instance: Which copy of the chip's internal data fabric the custom write targets; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Mask Value [63:48]: Top bits of the mask choosing which register bits the custom write may change; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Mask Value [47:32]: Upper-middle bits of the custom-write mask selecting changeable register bits; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Mask Value [31:16]: Lower-middle bits of the custom-write mask selecting changeable register bits; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Mask Value [15:0]: Bottom bits of the mask choosing which register bits the custom write may change; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Or Value [63:48]: Top bits of the value the custom write forces into the register; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Or Value [47:32]: Upper-middle bits of the value the custom write forces into the register; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Or Value [31:16]: Lower-middle bits of the value the custom write forces into the register; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Or Value [15:0]: Bottom bits of the value the custom write forces into the register; expert debugging only, leave default.
GRA Group 2: Turns on a second custom raw-register write rule for boot; expert debugging only, leave off.
GRA Group 3: Turns on a third custom raw-register write rule for boot; expert debugging only, leave off.
GRA Group 4: Turns on a fourth custom raw-register write rule for boot; expert debugging only, leave off.
GRA Group 5: Turns on a fifth custom raw-register write rule for boot; expert debugging only, leave off.
GRA Group 6: Turns on a sixth custom raw-register write rule for boot; expert debugging only, leave off.
Bootup NumLock State: Sets whether the keyboard's number pad starts on or off after the computer powers up.
Fast Boot: Skips some startup checks to boot quicker, but may not detect every device each time.
SATA Support: Chooses whether to initialize only the last boot drive or all connected SATA drives at startup.
VGA Support: Picks how the screen is brought up at boot; Auto suits most setups.
USB Support: Controls how thoroughly USB devices are set up during boot; full init is the safe choice.
PS2 Devices Support: Enables old-style round-connector keyboards and mice; leave on if you use one.
Redirection Support: Lets boot-time screen and keyboard be sent over a serial console for remote management.
NetWork Stack Driver Support: Enables network booting (loading the OS over the network); turn on only if you need PXE/network boot.
Onboard PCIE LAN PXE ROM: Lets the built-in network port boot the machine over the network; leave off unless you use PXE.
AMD CRB EHCI Debug port switch: Turns a USB port into a low-level debug link for developers; leave off for normal use.
Fan Setting: Selects the cooling-fan behavior: a custom curve, a balanced standard mode, or always full speed.
Temperature 1: Shows a live temperature reading from one of the board's sensors; this is a monitor, not an adjustable setting.
Temperature 2: Live reading from a second on-board temperature sensor — view only, just for monitoring.
Temperature 3: Live reading from a third on-board temperature sensor — view only, just for monitoring.
Temperature 4: Live reading from a fourth on-board temperature sensor — view only, just for monitoring.
Critical Temperature: The danger temperature at which the system shuts down to protect itself from overheating.
Fan Speed for Temperature 1: How fast the fan spins in response to the first temperature sensor's reading.
Fan Speed for Temperature 2: How fast the fan spins in response to the second temperature sensor's reading.
Fan Speed for Temperature 3: How fast the fan spins in response to the third temperature sensor's reading.
Fan Speed for Temperature 4: How fast the fan spins in response to the fourth temperature sensor's reading.
PSS Support: Lets the OS adjust CPU speed and power on the fly to save energy. Best left enabled.
PSTATE Adjustment: Picks which CPU speed/power level to tune — an advanced tuning knob, leave default.
PPC Adjustment: Caps the top CPU performance level the OS is allowed to request — advanced, leave default.
SVM Mode: Turns on hardware support for running virtual machines (KVM, VirtualBox). Enable to use VMs.
NX Mode: Marks data memory as non-executable to block many malware attacks — leave enabled.
C6 Mode: Lets idle CPU cores enter a deep sleep to save power and run cooler. Best left on.
CPB Mode: Allows the CPU to boost above its base speed under load. Leave on for performance.
Bank Interleaving: Spreads memory access across banks for better speed. Leave enabled.
Channel Interleaving: Spreads data across memory channels to boost bandwidth. Leave enabled.
Memory Clock: Sets the RAM speed; Auto picks a safe value automatically. Leave on Auto unless tuning.
Memory Clear: Wipes RAM contents on boot for privacy; slows startup. Usually left off.
Above 4G Decoding: Lets devices use memory above the 4GB mark — needed for big GPUs and passthrough. Enable.
SR-IOV Support: Lets one device split itself into virtual pieces shared among VMs. Enable only if needed.
PCI Latency Timer: How long a PCI device may hold the bus before yielding — advanced, leave default.
VGA Palette Snoop: A legacy fix for old multi-graphics-card color issues. Almost always leave off.
PERR# Generation: Lets devices report data-parity (corruption) errors on the bus — debugging aid, leave default.
SERR# Generation: Lets devices report serious system errors on the bus — debugging aid, leave default.
PCI-X Latency Timer: Bus-hold time for older PCI-X cards — rarely relevant today, leave default.
Relaxed Ordering: Lets devices reorder data transfers for speed. Leave default unless troubleshooting.
Extended Tag: Lets a device track more outstanding transfers at once for throughput. Leave default.
No Snoop: Lets devices skip a cache-consistency check for speed. Leave default unless tuning.
Maximum Payload: Largest chunk a PCIe device sends per transfer; Auto picks a safe size. Leave on Auto.
Maximum Read Request: Largest amount a PCIe device may ask for in one read; Auto is safe. Leave on Auto.
ASPM Support: Lets idle PCIe links power down to save energy; Auto is safe. Disable if a device acts up.
Extended Synch: Adds extra link sync patterns to help hardware analyzers — for debugging only, leave default.
Link Training Retry: How many times to retry bringing up a PCIe link before giving up — advanced, leave default.
Link Training Timeout (uS): How long to wait for a PCIe link to come up, in microseconds — advanced, leave default.
Unpopulated Links: Whether to keep empty PCIe slots powered on; turning off saves a little energy. Leave default.
Restore PCIE Registers: Restores PCIe settings after sleep so devices wake correctly. Leave enabled.
Don't Reset VC-TC Mapping: Keeps custom PCIe traffic-priority settings from being reset — advanced, leave default.
OnChip SATA Channel: Turns the built-in SATA controller on or off for hard drives and SSDs. Enable to use drives.
OnChip SATA Type: How SATA drives are presented to the OS (AHCI is the modern default). Use AHCI normally.
RAID Type: Picks the RAID layout when combining drives for redundancy or speed — only if using RAID.
OnChip IDE mode: Selects legacy vs modern addressing for the old IDE interface — rarely changed, leave default.
SATA IDE Combined Mode: Mixes SATA and old IDE ports together for legacy compatibility. Usually leave default.
XHCI Controller 0: Turns on the first USB 3.0 controller for fast USB ports. Leave enabled.
XHCI Controller 1: Turns on the second USB 3.0 controller for fast USB ports. Leave enabled.
OHCI HC(Bus 0 Dev 18 Fn 0): Enables a legacy USB 1.1 controller for very old USB devices. Leave enabled.
EHCI HC(Bus 0 Dev 18 Fn 2): Enables a USB 2.0 controller for standard USB devices. Leave enabled.
OHCI HC(Bus 0 Dev 22 Fn 0): Enables a second legacy USB 1.1 controller for very old devices. Leave enabled.
EHCI HC(Bus 0 Dev 22 Fn 2): Enables a second USB 2.0 controller for standard USB devices. Leave enabled.
USB PORT 0: Turns physical USB port 0 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 1: Turns physical USB port 1 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 2: Turns physical USB port 2 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 3: Turns physical USB port 3 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 4: Turns physical USB port 4 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 11: Turns physical USB port 11 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 12: Turns physical USB port 12 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
USB PORT 13: Turns physical USB port 13 on or off. Disable a port only to lock it down.
XHCI0 PORT 1: Turns on a specific USB 3.0 port on the first controller. Leave enabled.
XHCI1 PORT 0: Turns on a specific USB 3.0 port on the second controller. Leave enabled.
XHCI1 PORT 1: Turns on another USB 3.0 port on the second controller. Leave enabled.
SB GPP Function: Enables the chipset's general-purpose PCIe ports used for add-on devices. Leave enabled.
GPP Port Link Configuration: Splits the chipset PCIe lanes among slots (e.g. one x4 or four x1) — advanced, leave default.
GPP Link ASPM: Lets these chipset PCIe links sleep when idle to save power. Leave default.
GPP Gen2: Lets chipset PCIe ports run at the faster Gen2 speed. Leave enabled for full speed.
UMI Gen2: Runs the link between the CPU and chipset at faster Gen2 speed. Leave enabled.
GPP HW Compliance Mode: Puts a PCIe port into a test pattern for lab compliance testing — for engineers only, leave off.
SB GPP LANE REVERSAL: Flips the order of chipset PCIe lanes for certain board wiring — advanced, leave default.
UMI PHY PLL Power Down: Lets the CPU-to-chipset link's clock circuit sleep to save power — advanced, leave default.
SB GPP PHY PLL Power Down: Lets the chipset PCIe clock circuit sleep to save power — advanced, leave default.
IR Config: Sets up the old infrared remote/data port and which transmit line it uses. Leave off if unused.
SD MODE: Picks how the SD card slot moves data: smarter direct-memory transfer methods are faster, plain PIO is slowest. Auto/default is safest.
SD Trace Length: Tells the board how long the wiring to the SD slot is, so it can tune signal timing. Match your hardware or leave default.
SD SPEED: Sets whether the SD card slot runs at a slower, more compatible speed or a faster one.
SD BitWidth: Sets how wide a chunk of data the SD controller moves at once; wider can be faster. Leave default unless told otherwise.
HD Audio Azalia Device: Turns the built-in HD audio (the chip driving your headphone/speaker jacks) on or off. Auto is fine.
Azalia Front Panel: Enables the front-panel audio jacks (the headphone/mic ports on the case front). Auto is fine for most builds.
SDIN0 Pin Config: Sets whether this audio-chip pin works as an audio link or as a general-purpose signal pin. Advanced; leave default.
SDIN1 Pin Config: Sets whether this audio-chip pin works as an audio link or as a general-purpose signal pin. Advanced; leave default.
SDIN2 Pin Config: Sets whether this audio-chip pin works as an audio link or as a general-purpose signal pin. Advanced; leave default.
SDIN3 Pin Config: Sets whether this audio-chip pin works as an audio link or as a general-purpose signal pin. Advanced; leave default.
IMC Fan Control: Lets the board's tiny management chip control the fan speed automatically, or hand control to a hardware jumper.
CPU Thermal Zone 1: A temperature threshold the fan controller watches to decide when to start ramping the CPU fan up. Advanced tuning.
CPU Thermal Zone 2: A second temperature threshold for fan control, usually for a hotter stage of cooling. Advanced tuning; leave default.
CPU Hysteresis: A temperature buffer that stops the fan from constantly speeding up and slowing down near a threshold. Leave default.
CPU Fan PWM Stepping %: How big each fan-speed jump is when the controller adjusts the CPU fan. Smaller steps feel smoother. Advanced tuning.
CPU Fan PWM Ramping Rate: How quickly the CPU fan speeds up or slows down as temperature changes. Advanced fan-tuning; leave default.
CPU _AC0: A CPU temperature point that maps to a target fan speed in the cooling curve. Advanced fan tuning; leave default.
CPU _AC1: A CPU temperature point that maps to a target fan speed in the cooling curve. Advanced fan tuning; leave default.

Cooler and Quieter operation: Lets the CPU lower its speed and power when idle so it runs cooler and quieter. Best left on.
USB mode: Forces USB ports to a slower, more compatible speed, or lets the system pick automatically. Auto is best.
VCORE Fix Voltage: Locks the main CPU voltage to a value you set instead of letting the chip manage it. For advanced tuners only.
Voltage(mV): The fixed CPU core voltage, in millivolts, used when voltage is locked manually. Wrong values can crash or harm the chip.
VCORE Loadline: Tunes how much the CPU voltage sags under heavy load to keep it steady. Advanced overclocking knob; leave default.
GFX Fix Voltage: Locks the graphics part's voltage to a value you set instead of automatic control. For advanced tuners only.
GFX Loadline: Tunes how much the graphics voltage sags under heavy load to keep it stable. Advanced tuning; leave default.
MEMIO Fix Voltage: Locks the voltage for the memory-interface circuitry instead of letting the system manage it. Advanced; leave default.
Voltage: The fixed voltage for the memory-interface circuitry when set manually. Wrong values can cause instability; leave default.
